identity.name: Score
identity.definition: A Score is the measured outcome of a student's performance on an assessment or assignment — including scaled scores, percentiles, proficiency bands, growth measures, and skill-level breakdowns — that informs instructional decisions, tracks progress over time, and feeds platform recommendations.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How a Score appears in dashboards and score grids

attributes.description: The primary numeric result. Meaning depends on Score Type.

attributes.name: Score Type
attributes.description: Scaled, Percentile, Proficiency, Growth, or Percent.

attributes.name: Proficiency Band
attributes.description: At/Above, On Watch, Intervention, or Urgent.

attributes.name: Student
attributes.description: The student who earned this score.

attributes.name: Assessment/Assignment
attributes.description: The assessment or assignment this score belongs to.

attributes.name: Date
attributes.description: When the score was recorded.

attributes.name: Score Value
attributes.description: The primary numeric result. Meaning depends on Score Type.

attributes.name: Score Type
attributes.description: Scaled, Percentile, Proficiency, Growth, or Percent.

attributes.name: Proficiency Band
attributes.description: At/Above, On Watch, Intervention, or Urgent.

attributes.name: Student
attributes.description: The student who earned this score.

attributes.name: Assessment/Assignment
attributes.description: The assessment or assignment this score belongs to.

attributes.name: Date
attributes.description: When the score was recorded.

attributes.name: Growth SGP
attributes.description: Student Growth Percentile. Compares growth to similar peers.

attributes.name: Percentile Rank
attributes.description: National percentile rank. Compares to norming population.

attributes.name: Grade Equivalent
attributes.description: Grade-level equivalent for the score. e.g. 4.2 = 4th grade, 2nd month.

attributes.name: Duration
attributes.description: Time in minutes to complete the assessment.

attributes.name: Skill Breakdown
attributes.description: Per-skill proficiency levels derived from item responses.

attributes.name: Class
attributes.description: The class context in which this score was generated.

attributes.name: Item-Level Data
attributes.description: Per-item correct/incorrect and time-on-task. Available for diagnostic views.

stories.title: Review Student Score
stories.benefit: I can understand how the student performed, identify proficiency gaps, and decide on next instructional steps.
stories.whenClause: a student completes a Star assessment,
stories.thenClause: their Score appears with scaled value, proficiency band, percentile rank, and skill breakdown so I can quickly triage.

stories.title: Track Growth Over Time
stories.benefit: I can evaluate whether a student's interventions are working and adjust my teaching strategy accordingly.
stories.whenClause: I compare multiple Scores for a student across assessment windows,
stories.thenClause: the growth SGP and scaled score trend are visualized so I can see trajectory at a glance.

stories.title: Share Score with Parent
stories.benefit: parents can understand their child's performance and support learning at home.
stories.whenClause: I share a Score report with a parent,
stories.thenClause: a parent-friendly summary with proficiency band, grade equivalent, and growth context is sent.

businessRules.title: Immutability
businessRules.description: Scores are append-only. Once generated, a Score cannot be edited or deleted — only invalidated by an Admin with documented justification.

businessRules.title: Ownership
businessRules.description: Every Score belongs to exactly one Student and exactly one Assessment or Assignment. These references are set at creation and never change.

businessRules.title: Proficiency Calculation
businessRules.description: Proficiency bands are derived from scaled scores using grade-level benchmarks configured at the district level. Bands: At/Above, On Watch, Intervention, Urgent.

businessRules.title: Growth Computation
businessRules.description: Student Growth Percentile (SGP) requires at least two Scores from the same assessment family within the same school year. SGP ranges from 1-99.

businessRules.title: Recency
businessRules.description: The most recent Score for a given assessment type is the 'active' Score used in dashboards and recommendations. Historical Scores remain accessible for trend analysis.

businessRules.title: Access Control
businessRules.description: Students can view their own Scores. Teachers see Scores for students in their classes. Admins see all Scores within their school or district.

businessRules.title: Invalidation
businessRules.description: An Admin can mark a Score as Invalidated (e.g. testing irregularity). Invalidated Scores are excluded from proficiency calculations and growth measures but remain in the historical record.

states.name: Active
states.description: Current, valid score used in dashboards and calculations

states.name: Historical
states.description: Superseded by a newer score but retained for trend analysis
states.triggers: A newer Score of the same assessment type is recorded

states.name: Invalidated
states.description: Excluded from calculations due to testing irregularity

relationships.mechanics: A Score is system-generated when a Student completes an Assessment. The Student reference is set at creation and is immutable.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. Each Score belongs to exactly one Student; a Student accumulates many Scores over time.
relationships.sorts: Scores for a Student: by date (most recent first, default), by assessment type, by score value, by proficiency band.
relationships.filters: By assessment type (Star Reading, Star Math, AR), by proficiency band, by date range, by score type (Scaled, Percentile, Growth).
relationships.dependencies: If a Student is deactivated, their Scores persist for historical reporting but are excluded from active class-level aggregations.

relationships.mechanics: A Score is the output of an Assessment completion. Each Score references exactly one Assessment instance.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-one. Each Score belongs to one Assessment; an Assessment can produce many Scores (one per student).
relationships.sorts: Scores within an Assessment: by student name (default), by score value (descending), by proficiency band.
relationships.filters: By proficiency band, by growth percentile range, by class, by demographic group.
relationships.dependencies: If an Assessment is deleted, associated Scores are orphaned but retained. Assessment metadata is cached on the Score for display.

relationships.mechanics: Assessment engines decompose Scores into per-Skill proficiency levels based on item-level responses. This mapping is system-generated.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. A Score may map to multiple Skill proficiency data points; each Skill aggregates data from many Scores.
relationships.sorts: Skills within a Score: by domain (default), by proficiency level, by percent correct.
relationships.filters: By proficiency level (Proficient, Developing, Beginning), by domain/strand.
relationships.dependencies: Skill proficiency data depends on having item-level data. If item data is unavailable, skill breakdown is empty.

nestedObjects.name: Skill Mastery
nestedObjects.description: Per-skill proficiency level derived from item-level responses within this score

nestedObjects.name: Item Response
nestedObjects.description: Individual item correct/incorrect with time-on-task data

allAttributes.name: Score Value
allAttributes.description: The primary numeric result. Interpretation depends on Score Type (scaled score, percentile, percent correct, etc.).

allAttributes.name: Score Type
allAttributes.description: Categorizes the score: Scaled, Percentile, Proficiency, Growth, or Percent. Determines how the value is interpreted.

allAttributes.name: Student
allAttributes.description: The student who earned this score. Set at creation, immutable.

allAttributes.name: Assessment/Assignment
allAttributes.description: The assessment or assignment this score belongs to.

allAttributes.name: Date
allAttributes.description: When the score was recorded. Typically the assessment completion timestamp.

allAttributes.name: Proficiency Band
allAttributes.description: Performance band based on district-configured benchmarks: At/Above, On Watch, Intervention, Urgent.

allAttributes.name: Growth SGP
allAttributes.description: Student Growth Percentile (1-99). Requires at least two Scores from the same assessment family.

allAttributes.name: Percentile Rank
allAttributes.description: National percentile rank compared to norming population. Range 1-99.

allAttributes.name: Grade Equivalent
allAttributes.description: Grade-level equivalent (e.g. 4.2 = 4th grade, 2nd month). Star assessments only.

allAttributes.name: Skill Breakdown
allAttributes.description: Per-skill proficiency levels and percent correct, derived from item-level analysis.

allAttributes.name: Class
allAttributes.description: The class context in which this score was generated.

allAttributes.name: Duration
allAttributes.description: Time in minutes the student spent completing the assessment.

allAttributes.name: Item-Level Data
allAttributes.description: Per-item correct/incorrect, response time, and distractor analysis. Powers skill breakdown and diagnostic views.

structure.evidence: A Score has a rich attribute set: value, type, proficiency band, growth percentile, grade equivalent, skill breakdown, item-level data, and duration. You can design a full detail page for a Score.

instances.evidence: Star Reading 742 SS for Harry Potter (Mar 12, 2026), Star Math 215 SS for Ron Weasley (Mar 8, 2026), AR Quiz 90% for Hermione Granger (Feb 28, 2026). Schools generate thousands of Score instances per assessment window.

purpose.evidence: Scores are the primary output of assessments and the foundation for instructional decision-making. Teachers view Scores to triage students, track growth, group by proficiency, and assign follow-up work. Admins use Scores for school and district reporting.

sipValidation.verdict: Core data object. The essential output of assessment and the primary driver of instructional decisions across all Renaissance products.

synonyms.notes: Common in assignment and practice contexts where 'score' feels too formal
Overloaded — also means grade level. Avoid in product UI to prevent confusion
Used as a category label ('Student Performance') rather than individual score
Technical term for a score within a measurement framework
Used when Scores are treated as elements of a dataset rather than standalone objects
British English equivalent. Not used in US-focused products

shapeshifterMatrix.description: A student's view of their own score — the value, proficiency band, and trend compared to their last attempt. Communicates what the score means, not just what it is.
shapeshifterMatrix.userIntent: What did I score and what does it mean?

trendDirection.description: Trend compared to prior: up, down, or flat

shapeshifterMatrix.description: Scores for each student in a class on a specific assessment, with growth and percentile data. Teachers compare performance, identify struggling students, and assign follow-up work.
shapeshifterMatrix.userIntent: How did my class perform on this assessment?

studentName.description: Which student (when embedded in class view)

shapeshifterMatrix.description: A student's scores over time, displayed as timeline cards showing trends and growth targets. Teachers track whether a student is improving, plateauing, or declining.
shapeshifterMatrix.userIntent: How has this student's score changed over time?

shapeshifterMatrix.description: Aggregated scores across a school or district with proficiency band distributions. Admins analyze performance patterns and benchmark against targets.
shapeshifterMatrix.userIntent: How are scores distributed across my school/district?

shapeshifterMatrix.description: A simplified score card for parents showing their child's result, proficiency level, and how it compares to the grade average. Designed for clarity over detail.

The complete score record — raw value, proficiency band, growth percentile, skill breakdown, and item-level data. Teachers and admins analyze a score from every angle, share results, or assign follow-up.
shapeshifterMatrix.userIntent: What is the full detail of this score?

shapeshifterMatrix.description: The latest score displayed as a compact bar within a student's profile — score value, assessment name, and proficiency band at a glance.
shapeshifterMatrix.userIntent: What is the latest score for this student?

shapeshifterMatrix.description: Scores plotted for growth analysis with SGP, percentile rank, and dates. Teachers and admins assess a student's growth trajectory across assessment periods.
shapeshifterMatrix.userIntent: What is this student's growth trajectory?
